summary: I was born in the Philippines and I am proud to be a Filipina. Growing up there shaped my values and my close family ties. Leaving the Philippines in 2006 was difficult because I had to say goodbye to my loved ones, but I knew it was necessary for a better life. My memories and connections to the Philippines remain strong through pictures and calls to my parents.

I grew up in Abuyog, a province in Leyte, where life was simple and close-knit. Living there meant being surrounded by family and relatives, which I cherished deeply. The sense of community in Abuyog was very different from what I later experienced abroad. Leaving Abuyog was hard because it was my home and where my family was tightly connected.

Leyte is the island where Abuyog is located, and it was my home province in the Philippines. Life in Leyte was peaceful and centered around family and community. I left Leyte in 2006 to start a new chapter in the United States, but my heart remains tied to that place. I often think about Leyte when I look at photos of my family and remember my roots.

I arrived in the United States in March 2006, alone and for the first time traveling internationally. It was a long and nerve-wracking journey, but my faith and the support of my siblings and best friend helped me through it. I came here to be with my husband and to build a better life together. Since then, I have made the U.S. my home and live in Saint Paul, Minnesota.

Since arriving in the United States, I have lived in Saint Paul, Minnesota with my husband. We chose to stay here because of his work, and this city has become my new home. Although I miss my family back in the Philippines, Saint Paul is where I am building my life now. I keep my family close through photos and weekly calls, which help me feel connected despite the distance.
cues: lived in Saint Paul since arrival
